headline: Bail reform stalls with 14,000 remand prisoners awaiting trial
context: As of December 2024, approximately 14,000 prisoners were held on remand awaiting trial, many unable to afford bail amounts set by courts.

cards.big: 14,000 remand prisoners await trial, many detained solely because they cannot pay bail
cards.sub: Remand population represents 19% of total prison population

cards.big: Criminal Procedure Code gives magistrates wide discretion on bail amounts
cards.sub: No guidelines ensure bail amounts are proportionate to accused's means

cards.big: Presumption of innocence under Article 5 undermined by prolonged pretrial detention
cards.sub: Average remand period is 8 months before case reaches trial

cards.big: 83% of remand prisoners are from B40 income group per Prisons Department data
cards.sub: Poverty, not flight risk, is the primary reason for continued detention

cards.big: 14,000 people imprisoned for being poor, not for being guilty
Bar Council proposed bail reform guidelines to Chief Justice in October 2024
